question: Are eggs good or bad to eat, and what does the answer depend on?
shape: mundane-but-contested everyday question where framing the right sub-questions is half the work

sources.title: Examine.com — eggs and cholesterol evidence summary
sources.author: Examine.com
sources.role: disciplined claim-by-claim evidence grading (a model for the method itself)

challenge: Much of the difficulty is resolving WHICH sub-questions matter (dietary cholesterol vs serum cholesterol vs CVD outcomes; whole-population vs subgroup like hyper-responders) and what the appropriate ways of knowing are. The structure layer should make the sub-question decomposition explicit.
note: Add a few representative meta-analyses and a contrarian source to show the structure captures genuine subgroup-dependent disagreement rather than flattening it.
